Who I Am

ABOUT ME

I'm Bhavesh Rakesh Bhagat, a Bachelor of Technology student specializing in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering at Vidyalankar Institute of Technology, Mumbai. With a stellar CGPA of 8.8, I balance strong academic fundamentals with hands-on engineering experience.

My work sits at the intersection of hardware and software — from designing logic circuits and embedded systems to writing clean, functional code. I'm particularly passionate about VLSI design, RTL development, and IoT applications.

A quick learner with strong analytical instincts, I thrive in environments that challenge me to build real solutions to real problems.

What I've Built

PROJECTS

01

SMART BLIND STICK

Designed an Arduino-powered assistive device that alerts visually impaired users of obstacles within 30 cm using ultrasonic sensors. Integrates automatic control logic to improve mobility and independence.

Arduino Uno Ultrasonic Sensor Logic Circuits Embedded C
02

AUTOMATIC ATTENDANCE COUNTER

Built an IoT-based system that autonomously counts attendees for large gatherings using Arduino Uno. Eliminates manual counting overhead and improves accuracy for group events and institutions.

Arduino Uno IoT Sensors Automation
03

PORTABLE WATER FILTER

Created a low-cost filtration system treating water turbidity for personal emergency use. Applied basic chemistry principles to maintain potable water quality in flood and disaster scenarios.

Chemistry Fluid Mechanics Prototyping Emergency Systems
04

WATER BOAT PROTOTYPE

Designed and tested a buoyant prototype optimizing weight distribution and stability using fluid mechanics principles. Conducted flotation and stability testing to improve hull balance for enhanced maneuverability.

Fluid Mechanics Structural Design Team Project Testing
